Academic Profile | April 2026 Edition
Scholar | Educator | Research Leader

Zerihun Doda, PhD

Associate Professor of Sociology and Anthropology, Research Director, and Senior Faculty Member at Ethiopian Public Service University (EPSU)

I am an academic and researcher with more than 28 years of experience in teaching, research, training, consultancy, curriculum work, editorial leadership, and institutional reform. My work focuses on social protection, public service delivery, sustainable development, conflict and peace, climate change, qualitative research methods, and biocultural diversity conservation. This April 2026 edition expands my profile with fuller and more up-to-date academic, research, and public service details.

Based in: Addis Ababa, Ethiopia
Education: PhD in Anthropology & Biodiversity Management, University of Kent, UK
Research Profiles: Web of Science: V-7455-2018 | ORCID: 0000-0001-9545-8994 | Google Scholar: h-index 11, Citations 355 | Scopus and AD Scientific links are listed in the profile panel.

Career Timeline

A compact view of the major academic, institutional, and leadership phases of my career.

1996-2004
Dilla University Progressed from Graduate Assistant to Lecturer in Sociology and Anthropology, later coordinating research and publication work.
2004-2011
Hawassa University Taught widely across sociology, anthropology, policy, and development, while strengthening teaching, research, and public engagement.
2011-2015
University of Kent, United Kingdom Completed a PhD in Anthropology and Biodiversity Management with specialization in biocultural diversity conservation.
2016-2018
Return to Hawassa University Served as Assistant Professor, chaired journal-establishment work, and supported editorial leadership and interdisciplinary teaching.
2018-Present
Ethiopian Public Service University Associate Professor, Senior Faculty Member, Research Director, Editor-in-Chief, doctoral teacher, and national reform contributor.

Publications & Research

My profile brings together long-running work in sociology, social anthropology, public service studies, social protection, environment, peace and conflict, qualitative research methods, and academic leadership.

  • Research Fields Social protection, population dynamics, sustainable development, peace and conflict, climate change, traditional environmental knowledge, public service delivery, professional ethics, livelihood systems, identity, cultural heritage, gender social justice, and biocultural diversity conservation.
  • Current Research Portfolio Recent and ongoing studies include pro-environmental behavior in the manufacturing sector, child migrant laborers from Wolayta Zone in Addis Ababa, teachers' implicit ethnic-based bias, character strength and job performance, service delivery and citizen satisfaction, revenue-sector corruption, public sector greenness and cleanliness, institutional fitness and service efficiency, identity and diversity, and socio-cultural issues in environmental policy.
  • Publications Record This homepage now brings together journal articles, books and chapters, theses, commissioned studies, public reports, lecture notes, conference papers, posters, and curriculum-related materials from 2001 to the present.
  • Research Methods Profile Alongside subject-area scholarship, I have taught and facilitated advanced qualitative and mixed-methods research, especially NVivo, MAXQDA, and ATLAS.ti, for faculty, doctoral students, and researchers across several Ethiopian institutions.
  • Selected Recent Output Belda, F., Keno, G. I., Lemessa, A., & Doda, Z. (2025). The course of industrial development influencing smallholder farmers' peace in the face of structural and cultural violence. Journal of Rural and Community Development, 20(2), 125-147.

Courses Taught

Teaching spans doctoral, master's, and undergraduate levels, with strong emphasis on research methodology, social theory, anthropology, social policy, environment, and conflict studies.

  • Doctoral CoursesAdvanced Research Methodology; Advanced Qualitative Research Methods with CAQDAS; Advanced Research Methods; PhD Dissertation Seminar; Anthropology of Development; Seminars in Peace and Development; Contemporary Discourses in Gender, Peace and Sustainable Development; and Conflict and Climate Change in the Horn.
  • Graduate CoursesEcological Anthropology; Sociology of Conflict and Peace; Social Policy Analysis; Sociological Theories; Socio-Economics of Social Protection; Ethnicity and Ethnographic Analysis; Disaster Risk Management; Urban Sociology; Rural Sociology; Criminology; and Indigenous Knowledge Systems.
  • Institutions ServedEthiopian Public Service University, Addis Ababa University Center for Human Rights, Hawassa University, Haramaya University, Arba Minch University, and other partner institutions through doctoral teaching, advisory, and intensive methods training.
  • Software-Focused InstructionAdvanced hands-on training in NVivo, MAXQDA, and ATLAS.ti for PhD students, faculty members, and applied researchers.

Research, Reform & National Taskforces

Recent leadership includes university-level research management, national taskforces, institutional reform design, and public-sector consulting assignments.

  • Research DirectorSenior faculty leadership in research affairs at Ethiopian Public Service University since 2018, including strategy, systems strengthening, institutional initiatives, and research administration.
  • EPSU Reorientation StudyMember of the national reorientation study taskforce since November 2024, serving as secretary of the taskforce and editor-in-chief for the study report and policy brief production that helped redirect the University's future course.
  • Ethiopian Management InstituteContributor to the national taskforce on EMI reorientation, helping produce a high-level strategic report and long-range roadmap for the Institute's future direction.
  • Grand Reform LeadLeader of the reform project for the University's Research, Technology Transfer and Community Engagement division since November 2025, covering AS-IS analysis, TO-BE design, new structure, result- and competency-based job descriptions, competency assessment, and redeployment framework design.
  • Editorial LeadershipEditor-in-Chief of the Journal of African Development Studies since 2019, with ongoing work on journal systems, review processes, and publication quality.
  • Representative ProjectsCurrent and recent roles include enhancing pro-environmental behavior in the manufacturing sector, child migrant laborers from Wolayta Zone, service delivery and citizen satisfaction synthesis, structural fitness and efficiency in public organizations, green and clean work environments, and drivers of corruption in the Ethiopian revenue sector.

Program Design, Journals & Community Engagement

This strand of my work combines doctoral program development, journal establishment, academic service, and public communication.

  • Program DesignMember of the PhD Program Design Consulting Team in Transformational Leadership and Holistic Development for the Global Institute of Transformation Leadership since October 2024, with contributions to overall program design and course development.
  • Courses DesignedDesigned Advanced Research Methods, Anthropology of Development, and PhD Dissertation Seminars, and contributed to related master's-level holistic development curriculum work.
  • Journal EstablishmentMember of the establishment team for the International Journal of Transformational Leadership and Holistic Development, including editorial policy development, manuscript review workflow, and issue preparation.
  • Media & Public DialogueProfessional and voluntary media appearances in 2024-2025 on Addis Media Network and Finfine FM, discussing national reconciliation, kindergarten graduation culture, and solidarity-based social support during Ethiopian festive seasons, alongside earlier public engagement on sacred forest sites and conservation.

Doctoral Teaching & Examination

Advanced doctoral teaching, supervision, examination, and proposal review now form a substantial part of my academic service.

  • Doctoral Teaching ReachTaught advanced research methodology and advanced qualitative data analysis for PhD students at Ethiopian Public Service University, Addis Ababa University Center for Human Rights, Haramaya University, Hawassa University, and Arba Minch University.
  • PhD SupervisionSupervision and advisory work includes doctoral researchers such as Endris Jafer, Fikadu Belda, Zewdie Zakie, Teketel Adane, Mando Doyo, and Nigatu Ermias Kaltro.
  • Examination ServiceServed as external examiner, pre-viva examiner, and proposal reviewer for many PhD dissertations and master's theses across Ethiopian universities, including recent examining work for the University of South Africa (UNISA) in 2026 and Addis Ababa University in 2025.
  • Methods AdvisoryProvided continuing support in qualitative methodology, CAQDAS software use, dissertation development, and scientific report preparation for graduate researchers and faculty teams.

Training Facilitated

In addition to university teaching, I regularly facilitate research-methods and software trainings for faculty, graduate students, and public-sector researchers.

  • CAQDAS TrainingMAXQDA training at Hawassa University in 2022; ATLAS.ti training at Hawassa University in 2023; and repeated NVivo and CAQDAS trainings at Ethiopian Public Service University in March 2017 and December 2017.
  • Public Health Research TrainingATLAS.ti, CAQDAS, and research report writing support for Ethiopian Public Health Training Institute and Ethiopian Public Health Institute staff across 2019, 2021, 2022, and 2023.
  • University-Based Short CoursesATLAS.ti training for Ethiopian Policy University staff; Social Research Methodology and CAQDAS training for Wolayta Sodo University; and Grant Writing training for Bule Hora University in 2022.
  • Methods SpecializationTraining areas center on qualitative and mixed methods, coding workflows, analytical memoing, dissertation support, and software-supported research design.

Professional Development Received

Recent professional development has supported my teaching, editorial leadership, reform work, and university management roles.

  • Recent Certificates & WorkshopsDigital Learning and Pedagogy Certificate, Arizona University, 2024; Good Financial Governance Practices training, June 2024; and Catalyze Impact Initiative workshop on boosting African journal visibility, January 27-29, 2025, Addis Ababa.
  • Reform & Journal SystemsPublic-sector reform and competency-framework workshop with international and EU-supported expert teams in November 2025, and Online Journal Management training organized through Addis Ababa University Libraries and partner institutions in 2019.
  • Earlier DevelopmentEarlier professional development includes presentation skills, being assertive, networking, teaching with technology, time management, qualitative research refresher training, video skills, and population-health-environment training.

Curriculum Design & Review

Curriculum work has ranged from new program development to course design, review, revision, and modularization.

  • Transformational Leadership ProgramsPhD Program Design in Transformational Leadership and Holistic Development and related MA Holistic Development work for the Global Institute of Transformation Leadership, 2024-2026.
  • University Program ReviewReviewer and contributor for PhD Public Management work at Ethiopian Public Service University in 2025, MA Social Protection revision at Ethiopian Public Service University in 2024-2025, and MA Ecological Anthropology review work for Bule Hora University in 2021.
  • Earlier Curriculum ContributionsContributions to BA Anthropology and BA Sociology curricula, Social Work curriculum revision at Hawassa University in 2018, and modularization coordination work for southern universities in 2009.

Teaching Materials & Modules

The profile also includes course materials, reviewed modules, and training notes prepared for academic and professional audiences.

  • Core MaterialsIntroduction to Sociocultural Anthropology; Introduction to Sociology; Teaching Anthropology for Health Extension Workers; and Social Science Research Methods materials used in academic and training settings.
  • Methods ModulesAdvanced Qualitative Research Methods with CAQDAS: NVivo; Advanced Qualitative Research Methods with CAQDAS Supplement; and related research-methods modules prepared for graduate study and staff development.
  • Reviewed ModulesPopulation, Health and Environment module review work in 2004 and Ethnography module review work in the mid-2000s, alongside documentation and instructional materials prepared for anthropology teaching.

Conference Presentations

Conference papers and poster presentations have been an important part of sharing work on biodiversity, sacred sites, conservation, and social research.

  • International PosterPoster presentation at the University of Cambridge on the conservation of Podocarpus falcatus in sacred sites of Sidama during the PhD period.
  • National PapersPaper presentations recorded in the profile at Hawassa University in 2015, Mekele University in 2017, Jinka University in 2019, and Ethiopian Public Service University in 2021.
  • Recurring ThemesIndigenous knowledge, sacred natural sites, biodiversity conservation, livelihood resilience, and the relationship between culture and environmental stewardship.

Editorial & Reviewing Service

Editorial and peer-review work runs alongside research and teaching, especially in public health, social science, and interdisciplinary journals.

  • Journal LeadershipEditor-in-Chief of the Journal of African Development Studies; Associate Editor of the Journal of Police Science since 2022; and earlier Associate Editor for Hawassa University's Journal of Science and Development.
  • Peer Review ServiceReviewer roles include Web Ecology, BMC Public Health, Cogent Environmental Science, Cogent Social Sciences, SAGE Open, East African Journal of Social Sciences and Humanities, and ETHIOINQUIRY-related review work.
  • Editorial SystemsExperience includes manuscript handling, editorial policy design, reviewer coordination, and issue preparation for university-based and emerging journals.

Selected Research Projects

Research projects span university, public-sector, consultancy, and collaborative settings.

  • 2024-PresentEnhancing pro-environmental behaviors in the manufacturing sector; and child migrant laborers from Wolayta Zone in Addis Ababa.
  • 2023-2024Teachers' implicit ethnic-based biases toward students; and leveraging character strength for job performance enhancement.
  • 2021-2023Service delivery and citizen satisfaction in Ethiopia; public sector green and clean working environments and organizational productivity; and the state and drivers of corruption in the Ethiopian revenue sector.
  • 2019-2021Structural fitness and service provision efficiency in public-sector organizations; and socio-cultural issues in environmental policy formulation and implementation in Ethiopia.
  • Earlier WorkQualitative study of misuse of malaria protection bed nets in Adami Tulu, market-potential and livelihood studies, identity and diversity studies, endangered fish and ethno-ichthyological work, and commissioned culture and ethnohistory documentation projects.

Consultancy & Public-Sector Research

Consultancy work connects academic expertise with institutional reform, planning, policy design, and evidence-based decision making.

  • National StudiesFederal Civil Service Commission and related assignments on service delivery, citizen satisfaction, organizational culture, professionalism, work ethics, and strategic road-mapping.
  • Institutional ReorientationEthiopian Public Service University reorientation study and Ethiopian Management Institute reorientation taskforce work, each contributing to high-level institutional direction and future planning.
  • Applied ConsultingBansa Dyae Campus branch establishment socio-cultural and health impact assessment; consulting on policy, guidelines, and journal-related systems for education and training institutions; and continuing advisory support in qualitative methods and software-assisted research.

Academic Management & Committee Work

University service has included a broad range of academic leadership, policy, committee, and reform responsibilities.

  • Policy & SystemsChair and member roles in research and community engagement policy revision, anti-plagiarism policy work, sample-size framework development, senate legislation review, and strategic planning processes.
  • Research AdministrationLeadership in journal-management incentive schemes, report editing, reform design for research structures, and academic coordination for research, technology transfer, and community engagement.
  • Program & Event RolesContributions to anthropology program description work, graduation keynote preparation teams, academic taskforces, and institution-wide committee assignments linked to quality, governance, and reform.

Community & Voluntary Service

Public communication, student support, and voluntary academic service remain part of my broader professional contribution.

  • Media EngagementInterviews on Addis Media Network, Finfine FM, and Hawassa University Community Radio on national reconciliation, sacred forest conservation, children's graduation celebrations, and solidarity-based social support during festive seasons.
  • Student & Research SupportVoluntary soft-skills and life-skills sessions for school students, pre-viva and proposal examination service, and continued methods advisory for graduate students, faculty, and research teams.
  • Public ValueThese contributions reflect an effort to connect scholarship with public discussion, institutional learning, and practical social engagement.

Journal articles connected to my work, with links where a DOI, journal page, or public repository record is available.

Showing all visible records
  1. Belda, F., Keno, G. I., Lemessa, A., & Doda, Z. (2025). The course of industrial development influencing smallholder farmers' peace in the face of structural and cultural violence: The case of Lume District, Oromia, Ethiopia. Journal of Rural and Community Development, 20(2), 125-147.
  2. Fufa, Y., Desalegn, T. A., Abera, N., Getu, A., Tufa, Y. A., Demlie, Y. W., ... Doffana, Z. D., et al. (2025). Anthrax outbreak surveillance and response in Arba Minch, Ethiopia: After-action review. Journal of Public Health in Africa, 16(1), 1293.
  3. Belda, F., Imana, G., Lemessa, A., & Doda, Z. (2024). Land acquisition for industrial development and its repercussions on the peace of the local farming communities in Lume District, Oromia, Ethiopia. Cogent Social Sciences, 10(1), Article 2306031.

  11. Alemayehu, D., & Doda, Z. (2020). Indigenous environmental knowledge of Borana pastoralists. Grassroots Journal of Natural Resources, 3(4), 110-131.
  12. Doffana, Z. D. (2019). Sacred sites and ancestor veneration in Sidama, southwest Ethiopia: A socio-ecological perspective. Cogent Social Sciences, 5(1).

Books & Chapters

Selected books, chapters, and related public book records connected to my work.

  5. Doffana, Z. D. (2006). An Ethnographic Overview of Enset Producing Peoples of Ethiopia.

Public Studies & Institutional Reports

Public-facing studies, commissioned reports, and institutional research outputs linked to policy, reform, and applied inquiry.

  1. Ethiopian Public Service University Reorientation Study National Taskforce. Reorientation report and policy brief, with service as taskforce secretary and editor-in-chief for report production.
  2. Ethiopian Management Institute Reorientation Consulting and Research Taskforce. Strategic reorientation report and long-range roadmap.
